analysis.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully launched four astronauts from Kennedy Space Center on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, marking the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years. The mission, carrying three Americans and one Canadian aboard the Orion spacecraft atop the Space Launch System rocket, will conduct a 10-day lunar flyby without landing. The launch was briefly delayed by a technical issue approximately one hour before the launch window, which was resolved before liftoff at 6:35 p.m. ET.
